content.text: Hi this patient is not appearing in shopify – i believe their account was made through the new patient link

requestType.name: General Help Desk Request
requestType.description: Submit a Request or report and issue; fields below are based upon your selection.

content.text: We have assigned your request HEAL-33040 to an appropriate Help Desk team member. They will reach out to you shortly with an update.

This patient did not sync to Shopify automatically when his SF account was created. There’s a field  called Trig Shopify Sync that you are supposed to be able to set to True and it will sync the patient to Shopify. I tried that and it’s not working.

Can we check how this is configured and if something is broken? I think there might be a flow or some Apex code that syncs the patient to Shopify upon account creation. Any account created in SF should be synced to Shopify automatically. You would see an ID listed in the Shopify customer ID field and probably a message in the Shopify Sync Message field.
Our back-end way to sync accounts to Shopify appears to be broken. I’ve asked our SF team to investigate what is going on. I’m sorry, it might take a little while to track down this bug.
We haven't heard back from you for 24 hours, so we're automatically closing this support request.
Please feel free to open a new ticket if you feel this issues hasn't been fully resolved.
SELECT COUNT() FROM Account WHERE CreatedDate >= 2026-04-10T00:00:00Z AND Shopify_Customer_ID__c = null
706 accounts missing a Shopify Customer Id as of 5/22/26
This subflow runs Patient_Event_API and it returns zero data, can't capture Shopify customer ID
Subflow is launched from two locations:
Being disabled, it never calls the subflow
Calls same subflow but the subflow silently fails.
Testing confirmed (2026-05-22): Subflow debug (rollback mode enabled) with account 0010b00002LQU4xAAH
Flow completed without errors but returned no Shopify ID
See Shopify Customer ID field is empty. Shopify Sync Message field is empty.
Set Trig Shopify Sync field to True and save
No Shopify Customer ID is populated. No sync message is written. No visible error.
When an account is created on Trig Shopify Sync = true, the salesforce-to-shopify sync process shoulddo the following:
Create a Shopify customer record > populate the Shopify Customer ID field on the Account > write a success/failure message to Shopify Sync Message field.
